title: Keepers of the Ark vs. Keepers of the Truth
summary: “And the Philistines put themselves in array against Israel: and when they joined battle, Israel was smitten before the Philistines: and they slew of the army in the field about four thousand men. And when the people were come into the camp, the elders of Israel said, Wherefore hath the LORD smitten us to day before the Philistines? Let us fetch the ark of the covenant of the LORD out of Shiloh unto us, that, when it cometh among us, it may save us out of the hand of our enemies.” (1Samuel 4:2-3).     The Ark of the Covenant replaced faith in the God of Israel in the early days of Eli the High Priest. The Ark was worshipped rather than God. Keepers of the Ark are those who lose sight of the truth of God and focus on the ceremonial, traditional and ritualistic things associated with religion. With time, the children of Israel became keepers of the Ark but not Keepers of the Truth. In other words, they associated seeing the Ark of the Covenant with salvation and protection of God rather than the God who is omnipresent, omniscient and omnipotent; the God who cannot be reduced to a box made of acacia wood overlaid with gold. God hates this because it is no different from creating a new false religion.
